title: Stelis formicifera
taxonomy: Stelis formicifera is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This genus, Stelis, is a group of orchids that are typically small and delicate. The Stelis genus is further divided into several subgroups and species, each with unique characteristics. The classification of Stelis formicifera places it within the tribe Epidendreae, which is a large and diverse group of orchids. The exact classification can be further refined with more specific information about the species.

intro: Stelis formicifera, also known as Yellow Bee Orchid, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its yellow flowers that resemble the appearance of ants. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Stelis formicifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture or tea, and is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects.
